description:
## Problem
When `tokensPath` is null (the default), token tools (`get_design_tokens`, `find_token`) should NOT appear in the `tools/list` response. Currently `TOKEN_TOOL_DEFINITIONS` is unconditional. Once the server dispatcher is wired up, these tools would appear for all users even when no tokens file is configured — and every call would return an error.

This is bad DX: an AI agent will try to call `get_design_tokens` based on the tool list, only to receive "Token tools are disabled: tokensPath is not configured."

## Fix
In the server dispatcher (`src/index.ts`), when building the `ListTools` response:
- Only include `TOKEN_TOOL_DEFINITIONS` if `config.tokensPath !== null`

In `CallTool` dispatch:
- If `isTokenTool(name)` and `config.tokensPath === null`, return a clear error: "Token tools are not enabled. Set tokensPath in mcpwc.config.json or MCP_WC_TOKENS_PATH."

## Acceptance Criteria
- With `tokensPath: null` (default), `tools/list` does NOT include token tools
- With `tokensPath` set, `tools/list` DOES include token tools
- Integration test covers both cases
- Tool descriptions mention that tokens must be configured
title: Conditionally register token tools when tokensPath is null

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem
When `tokensPath` is null (the default), token tools (`get_design_tokens`, `find_token`) should NOT appear in the `tools/list` response. Currently `TOKEN_TOOL_DEFINITIONS` is unconditional. Once the server dispatcher is wired up, these tools would appear for all users even when no tokens file is configured — and every call would return an error.

This is bad DX: an AI agent will try to call `get_design_tokens` based on the tool list, only to receive "Token tools are disabled: tokensPath is not configured."

## Fix
In the server dispatcher (`src/index.ts`), when building the `ListTools` response:
- Only include `TOKEN_TOOL_DEFINITIONS` if `config.tokensPath !== null`

In `CallTool` dispatch:
- If `isTokenTool(name)` and `config.tokensPath === null`, return a clear error: "Token tools are not enabled. Set tokensPath in mcpwc.config.json or MCP_WC_TOKENS_PATH."

## Acceptance Criteria
- With `tokensPath: null` (default), `tools/list` does NOT include token tools
- With `tokensPath` set, `tools/list` DOES include token tools
- Integration test covers both cases
- Tool descriptions mention that tokens must be configured
